examples.title: CI rollout and platform constraints
examples.note: Numeric limits, alert preferences, and rollout state stay compact and queryable.
examples.transcript:
[2025-03-01] Dana: We have 20 concurrent jobs max on GitHub Actions right now. Keep Snyk Slack notifications aggregated and concise, not one alert per repo.
[2025-03-02] Ravi: Let's start with incremental scans and matrix jobs first, then widen coverage after the baseline stabilizes.
[2025-03-07] Dana: No mTLS yet. Put it in phase two after the canary rollout.
examples.later_question: What is the current concurrency limit, how should Slack alerts behave, and when is mTLS planned?
examples.answer_from_memory: The current GitHub Actions concurrency limit is 20 jobs. Slack notifications should be aggregated and concise. mTLS is planned for phase two after the canary rollout.

turns.speaker: Dana
turns.text: We have 20 concurrent jobs max on GitHub Actions right now. Keep Snyk Slack notifications aggregated and concise, not one alert per repo.
prism_memory: GitHub Actions concurrency limit: 20 concurrent jobs
Snyk Slack notifications aggregated and concise; no separate alerts per repo

Snyk Slack notifications aggregated and concise; no separate alerts per repo

turns.speaker: Ravi
turns.text: Let's start with incremental scans and matrix jobs first, then widen coverage after the baseline stabilizes.

memory_store_after_turn: GitHub Actions concurrency limit: 20 concurrent jobs
Snyk Slack notifications aggregated and concise; no separate alerts per repo

turns.text: No mTLS yet. Put it in phase two after the canary rollout.

Plan: start with incremental scans and matrix jobs; widen coverage after baseline stabilizes

examples.title: Trip updates and hard constraints
examples.note: Updated plans should replace stale ones while durable constraints remain.
examples.transcript:
[2025-06-12] Maya: We booked the Lisbon trip for September 14. I want a quiet hotel near Alfama, and no red-eye flights.
[2025-06-18] Omar: The outbound should leave after 9am because early flights wreck me.
[2025-07-02] Maya: Update the plan: Lisbon is off. We are going to Porto on September 21 instead, still no red-eye flights.
examples.later_question: Where is the trip now, on what date, and what flight constraint still applies?
examples.answer_from_memory: The current trip is to Porto on September 21, 2025, and red-eye flights are still off limits.

turns.text: We booked the Lisbon trip for September 14. I want a quiet hotel near Alfama, and no red-eye flights.
prism_memory: Prefers a quiet hotel near Alfama

Prefers no red-eye flights

examples.title: Health constraints and new routines
examples.note: Personal constraints, routines, and hobbies should stay available for later coaching or reminders.
examples.transcript:
[2025-08-03] Sam: My doctor wants me to keep sodium under 2 grams a day. I started painting on weekends because it helps me decompress.
[2025-08-17] Sam: I bought watercolors and signed up for a Saturday class downtown.
[2025-09-01] Sam: Skip late-night coffee from now on; it wrecks my sleep.
examples.later_question: What hobby did Sam start, what class did he sign up for, and what health constraints matter now?
examples.answer_from_memory: Sam started painting, signed up for a Saturday watercolor class downtown, should keep sodium under 2 grams per day, and wants to avoid late-night coffee.
